macro_events.event_summary: Trump declared the Iran ceasefire is "on life support" after rejecting Tehran's counter proposal, raising the risk of renewed Middle East military conflict.
primary_drivers: Geopolitical risk premium on crude oil from potential Strait of Hormuz disruption
Increased defense spending / munitions replenishment demand
Flight-to-safety bid for defensives and gold

beneficiaries.rationale: Largest US integrated oil major with significant upstream leverage; ~$1 move in Brent translates directly to earnings. Iran escalation threatens 20mb/d through Strait of Hormuz, supporting crude prices.
High-beta US shale pure-play (Permian) with heaviest oil-price sensitivity among large caps; tends to lead the group on geopolitical crude spikes.
Direct beneficiary of munitions replenishment (PAC-3, THAAD interceptors used against Iranian/proxy missile attacks on Israel and Gulf assets); already a primary supplier in prior escalation cycles.
Makes Patriot interceptors, SM-3/SM-6 (used by US Navy in Red Sea against Houthi/Iran-backed missiles); consumption-replenishment dynamic directly tied to active conflict.
B-21, missile defense, and ICBM exposure; benefits from broader defense budget tailwind if Iran conflict reignites, though less direct ordnance pull than LMT/RTX.
Diamondback — Permian pure-play with high oil-price torque; small-cap E&P names typically outperform majors on crude spikes.

losers.rationale: Jet fuel is ~25-30% of operating costs; Delta has the largest unhedged fuel exposure among major US carriers. Crude spike directly compresses margins.
Same jet fuel cost dynamic as DAL; international long-haul routes through Middle East airspace also face rerouting costs/closures.
Carnival — bunker fuel sensitivity plus discretionary travel demand risk; Mediterranean/Red Sea itineraries directly impacted by regional conflict.
Diesel/transport costs feed through to retail logistics margins; consumer discretionary spending also pressured if gasoline prices spike at the pump.
High-beta consumer discretionary with no oil hedge; risk-off geopolitical tape historically pressures momentum names disproportionately.

headlines: Trump says Iran ceasefire is 'on life support' after rejecting Tehran's counter proposal

macro_events.event_summary: Trump invites top US CEOs including Musk, Cook, and Fink to join him on a China trip for a summit with Xi, signaling a potential thaw in US-China commercial relations.
primary_drivers: De-escalation premium on US-China trade/tariff tensions
Potential market access deals for US firms with large China revenue exposure
Signal of constructive corporate diplomacy reducing decoupling tail risk

beneficiaries.rationale: Tim Cook personally invited; ~17-19% of revenue from Greater China and entire iPhone supply chain anchored there. Summit attendance signals protection from tariff escalation and possible regulatory relief on App Store/iCloud in China.

Musk attending; Shanghai Gigafactory is ~40% of global Tesla production and China is second-largest market. Any FSD approval progress or tariff/EV subsidy concessions discussed at summit directly drive earnings.

Fink invited; BlackRock has been seeking expanded access to Chinese asset management market (was first foreign wholly-owned mutual fund license holder). Summit could unlock further onshore distribution.

China export license regime for H20/compliant chips is core overhang; any softening of export controls discussed at CEO-level summit would directly restore a multi-billion dollar revenue line. Jensen Huang has lobbied openly for this.
Boeing aircraft orders are a traditional deliverable in US-China summits; China has withheld large-scale orders since 2017. A summit-tied order announcement is a well-established playbook.
Macau gaming concession holder with ~70% of EBITDA from China; benefits from any signal of warmer cross-strait/tourism normalization and reduced Macau regulatory pressure tied to diplomatic goodwill.
Roughly 60% of revenue from China handset OEMs; tariff/export-control de-escalation directly protects licensing and chipset shipments to Xiaomi, Oppo, Vivo.

losers.rationale: MP Materials' bull thesis depends on rare-earth decoupling from China; a US-China rapprochement weakens urgency of domestic supply chain subsidies and DoD price floors.
Defense primes carry a Taiwan/China-conflict risk premium; visible US-China diplomatic warming compresses that premium and slows urgency of Pacific deterrence supplementals.

Has lobbied for CHIPS Act protections premised on Chinese legacy-node overcapacity threat; trade detente reduces probability of additional Section 301 tariffs on mature-node chips that TXN has sought.

headlines: Trump invites Elon Musk, Tim Cook, Larry Fink and other CEOs to join China trip for Xi summit

macro_events.event_summary: April home sales came in weaker than expected as elevated mortgage rates continued to suppress buyer demand.
primary_drivers: Higher mortgage rates reducing affordability and transaction volume
Lower home turnover compressing volume-linked revenue (brokerage, title, mortgage origination, home furnishing)
Pressure on homebuilder order books and margin via incentives/rate buydowns

beneficiaries.rationale: Apartment REIT — weaker for-sale housing pushes marginal buyers into rentals, supporting occupancy and pricing power in coastal urban markets. Direct substitution effect.
Same rent-vs-buy substitution mechanism; AvalonBay's coastal multifamily portfolio benefits when high mortgage rates lock would-be buyers out of ownership.
Single-family rental operator — direct beneficiary of suppressed home purchases as households defer ownership and rent SFH instead.

losers.rationale: D.R. Horton's entry-level buyer base is the most rate-sensitive cohort; weak April sales signals continued need for costly rate buydowns, pressuring gross margins and orders.
Similar entry/move-up exposure as DHI; volume disappointment validates the demand-destruction narrative and pressures FY guidance.
Zillow revenue tied directly to transaction volume via Premier Agent and mortgage origination; lower sales = lower lead monetization.
Compass agent commission model — pure-play exposure to existing home sale volume; weak prints flow directly through revenue.
Rocket Companies' origination volume tracks purchase mortgage activity; persistent rate-driven weakness extends the origination drought.
Stewart/title peer — but using FAF: First American Financial title insurance revenue is direct function of home sale closings; April miss reinforces volume drought.
Home Depot — existing home sales correlate with remodel/big-ticket spend (~18 month lag from turnover); persistent weakness pressures Pro and DIY ticket. Comp guidance risk.

headlines: April home sales disappoint as higher mortgage rates weigh on buyers

macro_events.event_summary: US existing home sales rose in April but by less than economists expected, signaling continued sluggishness in the resale housing market.
primary_drivers: Weak resale turnover reduces realtor commissions and title/mortgage volume
Constrained existing supply diverts buyers toward new construction
Affordability strain implies mortgage rates remain a binding constraint

beneficiaries.rationale: D.R. Horton is the largest US homebuilder and a direct beneficiary when existing home supply stays tight — buyers unable to find resale inventory rotate into new builds, where DHI dominates the entry-level segment.
Lennar captures the same supply-substitution dynamic as DHI; weak existing sales has historically correlated with new-home order strength as resale listings stay scarce.
NVR's land-light model and concentration in mid-Atlantic where resale inventory is especially tight benefits when existing sales disappoint and buyers shift to new construction.

losers.rationale: Zillow's revenue is directly tied to existing home transaction volume via Premier Agent and mortgage origination leads; a softer-than-expected print pressures forward revenue assumptions.
Compass is a pure-play residential brokerage; commission revenue scales linearly with existing home sale volume, making it the most leveraged listed name to this print.
Opendoor's iBuying model depends on resale market liquidity to clear inventory; weak turnover lengthens holding periods and pressures gross margins.
Rocket Companies' purchase-mortgage origination volume tracks existing home sales closely; refi remains constrained so purchase weakness flows straight to revenue.
Angi's home services demand is partially tied to move-in/renovation activity around home transactions; weaker turnover crimps lead volume.

headlines: US existing home sales increase less than expected in April

macro_events.event_summary: Stocks rise and dollar holds steady ahead of US-China trade meeting, while Iran nuclear talks stall.
primary_drivers: Potential US-China tariff de-escalation reducing cost/revenue headwinds for China-exposed names
Iran talks stalemate keeps geopolitical risk premium in oil prices

beneficiaries.rationale: Roughly 90% of iPhones assembled in China; tariff truce or rollback directly removes a margin/COGS overhang and supply-chain relocation cost pressure.
Shanghai Gigafactory supplies ~half of global deliveries and China is its #2 market; de-escalation eases both export tariffs and local demand/political risk.
China export-license regime for H20/AI accelerators is a key bargaining chip; any softening of chip export controls expands addressable revenue (China was ~20%+ of data center).
Heavy machinery exporter with direct China end-market exposure and tariff cost on steel inputs; trade détente improves both volume and input costs.
Iran talks stalemate keeps sanctions on Iranian barrels in place, supporting Brent/WTI; XOM is the most direct large-cap beneficiary of a sustained geopolitical risk premium.
Pure-play US E&P with high oil-price torque; Iran sanctions persistence keeps marginal barrel out of market, supporting realized prices.

headlines: Stocks up and dollar steady ahead of US-China meeting, Iran talks hit stalemate
